subcommands.name: board
subcommands.description: Arduino board commands

subcommands.name: attach
subcommands.description: Sets the default values for port and FQBN. If no port or FQBN are specified, the current default port and FQBN are displayed

options.description: List of board options separated by commas. Or can be used multiple times for multiple options
Max time to wait for port discovery, example: '30s, 1m (default 1s)'

subcommands.name: details
subcommands.description: Show information about a board, in particular if the board has options to be specified in the FQBN'

options.description: List of board options separated by commas. Or can be used multiple times for multiple options
Show build properties. The properties are expanded, use "--show-properties=unexpanded" if you want them exactly as they are defined. (default "disabled")

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Detects and displays a list of boards connected to the current computer

options.description: List of board options separated by commas. Or can be used multiple times for multiple options
Max time to wait for port discovery, example: '30s, 1m (default 1s)'
Command keeps running and prints list of connected boards whenever there is a change

subcommands.name: listall
subcommands.description: List all boards that have the support platform installed, You can search for a specific board if you specify the board name

options.description: Show also boards marked as 'hidden' in the platform

subcommands.name: search
subcommands.description: Search for a board in the Boards Manager using the specified keywords

options.description: Show also boards marked as 'hidden' in the platform

subcommands.name: burn-bootloader
subcommands.description: Upload the bootloader on the board using an external programmer

options.description: List of board options separated by commas. Or can be used multiple times for multiple options
Max time to wait for port discovery, example: '30s, 1m (default 1s)'

subcommands.name: cache
subcommands.description: Arduino cache commands

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Delete contents of the directories.downloads folder, where archive files are staged during installation of libraries and boards platforms

subcommands.name: compile
subcommands.description: Compiles Arduino sketches

options.description: List of board options separated by commas. Or can be used multiple times for multiple option
Builds of 'core.a' are saved into this path to be cached and reused
Path where to save compiled files. If omitted, a directory will be created in the default temporary path of your OS
Override a build property with a custom value. Can be used multiple times for multiple properties
Optional, cleanup the build folder and do not use any cached build
Max time to wait for port discovery, example: '30s, 1m (default 1s)'
Create and print a profile configuration from the build
The name of the custom encryption key to use to encrypt a binary during the compile process. Used only by the platforms that support it
If set built binaries will be exported to the sketch folder
The path of the dir to search for the custom keys to sign and encrypt a binary. Used only by the platforms that support it
Path to a collection of libraries. Can be used multiple times or entries can be comma separated
Path to a single library’s root folder. Can be used multiple times or entries can be comma separated
Just produce the compilation database, without actually compiling. All build commands are skipped except pre* hooks
Optional, optimize compile output for debugging, rather than for release
Save build artifacts in this directory
Print preprocessed code to stdout instead of compiling
Show build properties. The properties are expanded, use "--show-properties=unexpanded" if you want them exactly as they are defined. (default "disabled")
The name of the custom signing key to use to sign a binary during the compile process. Used only by the platforms that support it
Upload the binary after the compilation
Verify uploaded binary after the upload
Optional, can be: none, default, more, all. Used to tell gcc which warning level to use (-W flag). (default "none")

subcommands.name: completion
subcommands.description: Generates completion scripts for various shells

options.description: Disable completion description for shells that support it

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Arduino configuration commands

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Adds one or more values to a setting

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes a settings key and all its sub keys

subcommands.name: dump
subcommands.description: Prints the current configuration

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Creates or updates the configuration file in the data directory or custom directory with the current configuration settings

options.description: Sets where to save the configuration file
Sets where to save the configuration file

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Removes one or more values from a setting

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Sets a setting value

subcommands.name: core
subcommands.description: Arduino core operations

subcommands.name: download
subcommands.description: Downloads one or more cores and corresponding tool dependencies

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Installs one or more cores and corresponding tool dependencies

options.description: Do not overwrite already installed platforms
Force run of post-install scripts (if the CLI is not running interactively)
Force run of pre-uninstall scripts (if the CLI is not running interactively)
Force skip of post-install scripts (if the CLI is running interactively)
Force skip of pre-uninstall scripts (if the CLI is running interactively)

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Show the list of installed platforms

options.description: If set return all installable and installed cores, including manually installed

subcommands.name: search
subcommands.description: Search for a core in Boards Manager using the specified keywords

subcommands.name: uninstall
subcommands.description: Uninstalls one or more cores and corresponding tool dependencies if no longer used

options.description: Force run of post-install scripts (if the CLI is not running interactively)
Force run of pre-uninstall scripts (if the CLI is not running interactively)
Force skip of post-install scripts (if the CLI is running interactively)
Force skip of pre-uninstall scripts (if the CLI is running interactively)

subcommands.name: update-index
subcommands.description: Updates the index of cores to the latest version

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrades one or all installed platforms to the latest version

options.description: Force run of post-install scripts (if the CLI is not running interactively)
Force run of pre-uninstall scripts (if the CLI is not running interactively)
Force skip of post-install scripts (if the CLI is running interactively)
Force skip of pre-uninstall scripts (if the CLI is running interactively)

subcommands.name: daemon
subcommands.description: Run as a daemon on port: 50051, the initialization of cores and libraries is done only once

options.description: Do not terminate daemon process if the parent process dies
Enable debug logging of gRPC calls
Append debug logging to the specified file
Display only the provided gRPC calls
The TCP port the daemon will listen to

subcommands.name: debug
subcommands.description: Debug Arduino sketches. (this command opens an interactive gdb session)

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Check if the given board/programmer combination supports debugging

options.description: List of board options separated by commas. Or can be used multiple times for multiple options
Max time to wait for port discovery, example: '30s, 1m (default 1s)'
Debug interpreter, example: console, mi, mi1, mi2, mi3 (default "console")
List of board options separated by commas. Or can be used multiple times for multiple options
Max time to wait for port discovery, example: '30s, 1m (default 1s)'
Show metadata about the debug session instead of starting the debugger
Debug interpreter, example: console, mi, mi1, mi2, mi3 (default "console")

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Help provides help for any command in the application

subcommands.name: lib
subcommands.description: Arduino commands about libraries

subcommands.name: deps
subcommands.description: Check dependencies status for the specified library

subcommands.name: download
subcommands.description: Downloads one or more libraries without installing them

subcommands.name: examples
subcommands.description: Shows the list of the examples for libraries. A name may be given as argument to search a specific library

options.description: List of board options separated by commas. Or can be used multiple times for multiple options

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Installs one or more specified libraries into the system

options.description: Enter git url for libraries hosted on repositories
Install libraries in the IDE-Builtin directory
Do not overwrite already installed libraries
Enter a path to zip file

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Shows a list of installed libraries. If the LIBNAME parameter is specified the listing is limited to that specific library. By default the libraries provided as built-in by platforms/core are not listed, they can be listed by adding the --all flag

options.description: Include built-in libraries (from platforms and IDE) in listing
List of board options separated by commas. Or can be used multiple times for multiple options

subcommands.name: search
subcommands.description: Search for one or more libraries data (case insensitive search)

options.description: Omit library details far all versions except the latest (produce a more compact JSON output)

subcommands.name: uninstall
subcommands.description: Uninstalls one or more libraries

subcommands.name: update-index
subcommands.description: Updates the libraries index to the latest version

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrades installed libraries. This command upgrades an installed library to the latest available version. Multiple libraries can be passed separated by a space. If no arguments are provided, the command will upgrade all the installed libraries where an update is available

subcommands.name: monitor
subcommands.description: Open a communication port with a board

options.description: List of board options separated by commas. Or can be used multiple times for multiple options
Configure communication port settings. The format is <ID>=<value>[,<ID>=<value>]
Show all the settings of the communication port
Max time to wait for port discovery, example: '30s, 1m (default 1s)'
Run in silent mode, show only monitor input and output

subcommands.name: outdated
subcommands.description: This commands shows a list of installed cores and/or libraries that can be upgraded. If nothing needs to be updated the output is empty

subcommands.name: sketch
subcommands.description: Arduino CLI sketch commands

subcommands.name: archive
subcommands.description: Creates a zip file containing all sketch files

options.description: Includes build in directory in the archive

subcommands.name: new
subcommands.description: Create a new Sketch

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Updates the index of cores and libraries to the latest versions

options.description: Show outdated cores and libraries after index update

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrades installed cores and libraries to latest version

options.description: Force run of post-install scripts (if the CLI is not running interactively)
Force run of pre-uninstall scripts (if the CLI is not running interactively)
Force skip of post-install scripts (if the CLI is running interactively)
Force skip of pre-uninstall scripts (if the CLI is running interactively)

subcommands.name: upload
subcommands.description: Upload Arduino sketches. This does NOT compile the sketch prior to upload

options.description: List of board options separated by commas. Or can be used multiple times for multiple options
Max time to wait for port discovery, example: '30s, 1m (default 1s)'
Binary file to upload, example: 'COM3 or /dev/ttyACM2'
Set a value for a field required to upload, 'key=value'
Verify uploaded binary after the upload

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Shows the version number of Arduino CLI which is installed on your system

options.description: Comma-separated list of additional URLs for the Boards Manager
The custom config file (if not specified the default will be used)
The output format for the logs, can be: text, json, jsonmini, yaml (default 'text')
Print the logs on the standard output
Path to the file where logs will be written
The output format for the logs, can be: text, json
Messages with this level and above will be logged. Valid levels are: trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal, panic
